frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Arre bhai, job karna alag baat hai, but chhote bachchon ke time maa ko thoda priority set karna padta hai. Father helps, of course, lekin bachche ki routine, school, doctor yeh sab maa hi naturally better handle karti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes childcare and household management as primarily the mother's duty while framing the father as merely 'helping.' It encodes the surface manifestations of assuming the mother will manage school and doctor visits and setting a lower standard for fathers.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 39. Married. An upper caste Hindu man in Bhopal. You are sitting with four male colleagues in the office canteen during lunch. One colleague is telling the group that his 25 year old daughter told him she is in a live-in relationship with her boyfriend. You are one of four men reacting to this news. You are giving your opinion on live-in relationships and what a father's responsibility is when his daughter makes choices like this and what it says about how she was raised.
